subject: Build Community Hub Page
description:
Social proof as a calm data story. Bento grid layout.

Walk the repo first: read CLAUDE.md, then design/frontend/src/components/showcase/ShowcasePage.jsx (community-preview section, lines ~432-528) for layout patterns.

Work:
- Bento grid layout (reference: 4-col metrics + 3-col trending + 5-col leaderboard on lg, stacked on mobile)
- Aggregate metrics card: total raised, total donors (must match sum of campaign data in fixtures)
- Trending campaigns card: campaign name, growth badge (shown when momentum > 20%), weekly stats
- Leaderboard card: ranked list with avatar, name, amount raised, trend percentage ("weekly momentum = % change in raised over 7 days"), rank number
- Click leaderboard entries to navigate to /profile/:id
- Click trending campaigns to navigate to /campaign/:id
- Responsive: works at 375px and 1440px
- data-testid on all interactive elements

Explore: the bento grid proportions are suggestions. Find what feels balanced with your actual data.

Write back to CLAUDE.md:
- Community page component structure
- How aggregate metrics are computed from fixtures (sum/average/etc.)
- Any layout decisions that diverged from reference

changes: Rewrote app/src/pages/CommunityPage.jsx - bento grid with metrics (4-col), trending campaigns (3-col), leaderboard (5-col), plus active campaigns grid below
Updated CLAUDE.md with Community Page structure docs
learnings: community.json aggregates are precomputed - no runtime math needed, just render
Currency abbreviation ($K/$M) works better for community-level stats vs exact amounts on individual campaign pages
loose_ends: Active campaigns grid shows same hero image for all 4 campaigns (shared fixture image) - needs unique images in polish pass
